axes.name: State-run Service Delivery ↔ Outsourced & Private
axes.description: Who delivers municipal services. Positive (+1): State employees run water, electricity, refuse, and road services directly. Negative (-1): Private contractors and concessions deliver services under performance contracts.

axes.name: Strict Financial Accountability ↔ Flexible Management
axes.description: Financial management and anti-corruption. Positive (+1): Strict audit culture, personal criminal liability for misconduct, independent oversight units. Negative (-1): Flexible management, political discretion over financial decisions.

axes.name: Formalise In Situ / Community Housing ↔ Relocate & Market
axes.description: Housing and informal settlement policy. Positive (+1): Formalise settlements in place, subsidised rental housing, oppose evictions. Negative (-1): Relocate to formal housing, market-led development, enforce land-use bylaws.

axes.name: Subsidised / Capped Tariffs ↔ Cost-reflective Rates
axes.description: Rates, tariffs, and service affordability. Positive (+1): CPI-capped tariffs, subsidised connections, expanded indigent rebates. Negative (-1): Cost-reflective, market-rate tariffs and strict disconnection enforcement.

axes.name: Transparent & Structured Coalitions ↔ Executive Flexibility
axes.description: Coalition politics at metro level. Positive (+1): Binding, public coalition agreements with accountability mechanisms. Negative (-1): Flexible, ad-hoc coalitions with executive discretion and informal deals.

axes.name: Mass Transit Investment ↔ Private Transport / Taxis
axes.description: Urban mobility and transit. Positive (+1): Investment in BRT, rail, cycling, and pedestrian infrastructure. Negative (-1): Accommodation of private transport, minibus taxis, and car-oriented infrastructure.

axes.name: Densification & Transit-oriented ↔ Greenfield & Sprawl
axes.description: Urban form and development. Positive (+1): Densification near transit corridors, inner-city regeneration, rezoning for higher density. Negative (-1): Greenfield peripheral development, suburban sprawl, heritage protection over density.

axes.name: Expanded Metro Police ↔ SAPS Reliance
axes.description: Urban policing and safety. Positive (+1): Expanded metro police forces, community policing forums with formal power. Negative (-1): Reliance on national SAPS, national policing primacy in cities.

axes.name: Participatory Ward Democracy ↔ Representative Council
axes.description: Democracy at ward level. Positive (+1): Ward committees with budget power, councillor recall, community referendums. Negative (-1): Councillor discretion, representative democracy without direct participation mechanisms.

axes.name: Active Municipal LED ↔ Private Sector-led
axes.description: Local economic development. Positive (+1): Municipal job creation, business incubators, local procurement preference. Negative (-1): Private sector-led growth, enabling environment only, minimal council intervention.
